Only summarize the core factual basis behind the correct answer choice based strictly on available data—no additional commentary or inference outside this scope.

Impression compounds have low thermal conductivity, which enables them to retain heat during dental procedures and helps maintain their form while being manipulated. This characteristic necessitates careful control over environmental temperatures when working with these materials, such as using warm water baths around 55-60°C to achieve proper consistency without causing premature hardening. 

Final Answer: \boxed{A}

Correct Summary (only two sentences; purely clinical relevance):

Impression compound bases exhibit low thermal conductivity, allowing them to retain heat and remain pliable during manipulation in the mouth—a critical factor ensuring accuracy in impressions. As a result, clinicians must carefully manage ambient temperature through warmed environments (typically 55–60°C) to prevent excessive cooling-induced stiffening before final placement.
